sourceMovie.title: The Platform
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Platform works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Elysium
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Platform clicked for you, Elysium (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Platform clicked for you, 10 Cloverfield Lane (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Platform?
faq.answer: Try Elysium, 10 Cloverfield Lane, The Survivalist—each shares DNA with The Platform while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Platform?
faq.answer: The Platform (2019) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Platform part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Platform Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
